headline: Hormuz Rally Day Two: Heard on the Street Recap
summary: Stocks rose, extending a “Hormuz Hope” rally from Tuesday.  President Trump plans to deliver a speech Wednesday at 9 p.m. ET to give “an important update on Iran,” the White House said.  Exxon Mobil shares fell 5%, their biggest percentage decline in almost a year.

headline: Exxon Mobil and Chevron Fall 5%: What Iran’s President Just Did to U.S. Oil Stocks
summary: Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) shares are down 5% in midday trading on Wednesday, and Chevron (NYSE:CVX) shares are also off 5%. The catalyst is hard to miss: reports that Iran’s President is prepared to end the ongoing conflict are unwinding the geopolitical risk premium that had driven oil prices to over $100 per barrel. Both stocks ... Exxon Mobil and Chevron Fall 5%: What Iran’s President Just Did to U.S. Oil Stocks

headline: Exxon Mobil on Pace For Largest One-Day Market Cap Loss Since 2008
summary: One of early 2026’s biggest successes in financial markets is off to a rough start in the second quarter.  Exxon Mobil has lost around $36 billion in market cap so far on Wednesday—on pace for its largest one-day market cap loss since the global financial crisis in 2008, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  The S&P 500 energy sector was also down amid investor hopes for an end to the war in Iran that would bring relief to oil prices.

headline: Exxon scientists warned management over algae biofuel doubts in 2020, WSJ says
summary: Exxon Mobil’s (XOM) scientists told one of the oil major’s top strategic-planning executives in February 2020 that its much-heralded algae biofuels programs was not close to hitting its stated goals, the Wall Street Journal’s Collin Eaton reports. The presentation was one of multiple communications to the company’s top management in early 2020 that even the most promising strains of algae were struggling to produce much oil outside the lab, the author says, citing documents.End of Quarter Sale -
